figures.caption: Figure 1 Four typical generation processes of implicit preference data, where “copy” represents user feedback. We denote the true user preference as positive (r∗ = 1) or negative (r∗ = 0), and the user interaction as active (a = 1) or passive (a = 0). Different colors indicate different r∗. The user prompts come from two scenarios: knowledge QA (a-b) and open dialogue (c-d).
Table 2 The overall performance on implicit preference datasets.
Table 6 Qualitative case study on HarmBench with Qwen2.5-7B steered by different reward models.
Table 4 Varying LLM backbone performance on PKU-SafeRLHF.
Figure 2 Performance comparison under different update rate η and batch size B on HelpSteer.
Figure 3 Performance comparison under different proportions of positive samples that elicit user actions (α).
